macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto market sees sharp selloff as more than $623 million in longs are liquidated
items.summary: Over the past 24 hours, a major leverage flush hit cryptocurrency markets, with more than $623 million in long positions reportedly liquidated amid sharp volatility. The move was tied to a broader risk-off environment and rapid price swings across major digital assets.

items.title: Crypto market loses about $100 billion in 24 hours as Bitcoin slips below $78,000
items.summary: A dramatic market downturn erased more than $100 billion in crypto market value within a day, with Bitcoin briefly falling below $78,000. The selloff also hit altcoins and came alongside heavy liquidation pressure across leveraged positions.

items.title: Dogecoin outperforms as futures volume surges and trading activity rebounds
items.summary: Dogecoin gained even as the broader crypto market weakened, with spot volume jumping sharply and futures volume climbing to a four-day high. The activity suggested renewed trader interest, while technical indicators pointed to potential further upside.

items.title: Crypto market loses $70 billion in hours amid leverage unwind and ETF outflows
items.summary: Another market drawdown wiped out roughly $70 billion in value as Bitcoin lost the $80,000 level and more than 100,000 traders were reportedly liquidated. The post also pointed to significant ETF outflows as a warning sign for institutional demand.

items.title: XRP ETFs attract $60.5 million in inflows while Bitcoin and Ethereum funds see outflows
items.summary: XRP-linked ETFs recorded their largest weekly inflow of 2026 at $60.5 million, contrasting with notable outflows from Bitcoin and Ethereum ETFs. The report also highlighted growth in XRP Ledger activity tied to tokenized real-world assets.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Subnautica 2 sells 2 million copies in 12 hours after Early Access launch
items.summary: Unknown Worlds’ Subnautica 2 has reportedly sold over 2 million copies within its first 12 hours on Early Access, with concurrent player counts peaking above 600,000. The launch has been one of the biggest gaming stories of the past day.

items.title: Nintendo adds most of Virtual Boy’s library to Switch Online
items.summary: Nintendo’s latest update has already brought most of the Virtual Boy game library to Switch Online, with five new titles available. It’s a notable expansion of the retro subscription service.

items.title: Lego 2K Drive set to be delisted on May 19
items.summary: Engadget reports that Lego 2K Drive will disappear from digital storefronts on May 19, while multiplayer servers will remain online for another year. The delisting is drawing attention because it comes only three years after release.

items.title: Best Buy affiliate email sparks GTA 6 pre-order speculation
items.summary: A Best Buy message suggesting GTA 6 pre-orders may begin next week has fueled speculation that Rockstar or Take-Two could be preparing new marketing. The timing has also raised hopes of a fresh trailer.

items.title: Two unannounced Xbox controllers surface online
items.summary: Images and details of two unrevealed Xbox controllers have appeared, including an Elite Series 3 model and a dedicated cloud gaming pad. Microsoft may be gearing up for new hardware accessories.

items.title: Metroid Dread studio MercurySteam announces layoffs
items.summary: MercurySteam, the Spanish developer behind Metroid Dread, has reportedly laid off staff. The news comes as the studio’s newest game is still scheduled to arrive on Steam soon.

items.title: Rumor: new inFAMOUS game may be in development
items.summary: A gaming news roundup circulated a claim that a new inFAMOUS game is reportedly in development, though the report is based on a single source and remains unconfirmed. At this stage it should be treated as a rumor rather than an announcement.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Google expands Gemini features, but only to select high-end Android phones for now
items.summary: Google’s latest Gemini Intelligence rollout appears to be limited to a small set of premium Android flagships at launch, underscoring a staged release strategy for its newest smartphone AI features. The development is notable because it affects access to a flagship software capability across the Android ecosystem.

items.title: Samsung begins One UI 8.5 stable rollout for Galaxy S24, Fold6, and Flip6 in the US
items.summary: Samsung is now delivering the stable One UI 8.5 update to several of its recent flagship devices in the United States. The rollout is a major software update for Galaxy owners and signals Samsung’s continued support cadence for its premium lineup.

items.title: Google tightens Gmail storage policies for some new accounts
items.summary: Google is reportedly requiring phone numbers for access to the full 15GB of Gmail storage on some new accounts, while others may receive only 5GB free. The change is drawing attention as a significant shift in Google account onboarding and storage access.

items.title: Windows updates may soon be able to automatically repair broken driver installs
items.summary: Microsoft is working on a feature that would allow Windows to automatically fix problematic driver updates. If widely deployed, it could reduce one of the most common sources of post-update system issues for PC users.

items.title: NGINX vulnerability reportedly exploited in the wild
items.summary: Security researchers say CVE-2026-42945 affecting NGINX is being actively exploited, with attacks causing worker crashes and possibly enabling remote code execution. The finding makes it one of the most urgent recent infrastructure security issues.

items.title: Grafana says GitHub token breach led to codebase download and extortion attempt
items.summary: Grafana disclosed that a GitHub token compromise enabled an attacker to download part of its codebase and attempt extortion. The incident is a notable example of supply-chain and source-control security risk affecting a major developer platform.

items.title: Meta launches private Incognito Chat mode in WhatsApp and Meta AI app
items.summary: Meta has introduced an Incognito Chat option intended for more private conversations within WhatsApp and the Meta AI app. The feature adds a new privacy-focused messaging option to Meta’s consumer apps.
